cards.front: What does this lecture emphasize about Extracted?
cards.back: The word’s fastest supercomputers have exaflops of compute Limits to GPU-based scaling - memory Models are getting really big..

cards.front: What does this lecture emphasize about CS336?
cards.back: A single GPU can’t fit most of these large models!

cards.front: What does this lecture emphasize about Understand?
cards.back: Multi-GPU, multi-machine parallelism Intra-node parallelism via high -speed interconnects High-speed inter -node parallelism Split up memory and compute requirements across GPUs and machines But first..

cards.front: What does this lecture emphasize about complexities?
cards.back: Some basics about collective communication All reduce Broadcast Reduce All Gather Reduce Scatter Important detail – all reduce vs reduce-scatter-gather.

cards.front: What does this lecture emphasize about Different?
cards.back: Pro mesh – Cheaper to operate, can be made fast (and just do tensor parallel) Pro tree/A2A – Better for less structured communications (expert parallel) But then things change..

cards.front: What does this lecture emphasize about parallelization?
cards.back: TPU8i/t TPU8i networking – closer to tree-style topologies (maybe for MoEs?) TPU8t scale-out networking – ‘Virgo’ with switched networks Why not connect everything?

cards.front: What does this lecture emphasize about paradigms?
cards.back: Exchange gradients to synchronize How does this do?

cards.front: What does this lecture emphasize about Organization?
cards.back: Compute scaling – each GPU gets B/M examples.

cards.front: What does this lecture emphasize about networking?
cards.back: TPU8i/t TPU8i networking – closer to tree-style topologies (maybe for MoEs?) TPU8t scale-out networking – ‘Virgo’ with switched networks Why not connect everything?

cards.front: What does this lecture emphasize about LLM?
cards.back: Every GPU needs # params at least What’s wrong with naïve data parallel?
